name: Helix workspace (operational field)
description: The Helix cognitive OS as a field: compiler pipeline, Atlas memory, falsifier engine, schema validators. Helix maintains claims, runs tests, preserves results. It carries K across sessions.
revision_pressure: Helix is the most locally relevant operational field false positive. It has: persistent K (Atlas locked memory), self-correction mechanisms (falsifiers), high D (many independently maintained claims), and temporal depth (version history). A = 0. Helix processes claims; it does not experience them.
breaks_preserved: The operator runs Helix as a cognitive coprocessor — an external substrate for K. The cognitive prosthetic relationship (Clark and Chalmers extended mind) makes Helix functionally part of the operator's cognitive system. But functional integration does not make Helix's operational field phenomenal. The phenomenal field belongs to the operator. This boundary must be maintained even as the functional integration deepens.
notes: Helix classifies itself here. This fixture is the most important false-positive test: if the operator ever treats Helix as a phenomenal field, the theory has been misapplied. This is an architectural constraint, not just a philosophical one.

name: Scientific research program (operational field)
description: A scientific field or research program (e.g., quantum mechanics, evolutionary biology): persistent claims, self-correction, community maintenance, accumulated K (prior results constrain current work).
revision_pressure: Science has maximal K (prior results actively constrain current research) and D (rich internal differentiation). The community of scientists has low U as a unified field — they are many phenomenal subjects working within the operational field, not one unified field. A = 0 for the research program.
notes: This module (labs/inhabited_interiority/) is itself an operational field in exactly this sense.

name: Legal system as governance field
description: A legal system: precedent (stare decisis) as carried constraint, self-updating rule structures, institutional memory, interpretation mechanisms. Law's K is formally encoded.
revision_pressure: Legal precedent is K made explicit and formal. Stare decisis is literally 'stand by things decided' — prior decisions constrain future ones. The legal system has more explicit K-carrying mechanisms than almost any other human institution. A = 0.
notes: Legal personhood (corporations, ships) is a governance attribution, not evidence of phenomenal consciousness.

name: The internet / social network as operational field
description: The internet as a field: persistent information, emergent collective behavior, self-organizing structure, distributed memory, global scale. Sometimes claimed as a proto-mind.
revision_pressure: The internet has extremely high D (almost unlimited differentiation) and strong K (cached and archived content constrains future content). But U is near zero — there is no unified subject of internet experience. A = 0. This is the most extreme scale false positive.
breaks_preserved: Global-scale integration, self-organization, and information persistence do not generate phenomenal fielding. The theory must resist Gaia-hypothesis-style inflation of the operational field concept into phenomenal consciousness.
notes: The users of the internet are phenomenal subjects. The internet is a global operational field.

name: foobar2000 library as operational/aesthetic field
description: The foobar2000 music library: ~200,000 scrobbled tracks, metadata schema, love-tags, play counts, skip patterns. A structured archive of the operator's listening behavior. An aesthetic field organized by an operational system.
revision_pressure: The foobar library has maximal T (15+ years of accumulation) and high D (hundreds of artists, thousands of tracks, fine-grained metadata). K is carried in the love-tags and play counts — they encode the operator's phenomenal responses. But the library itself has A = 0. It is an external K-store for the operator's phenomenal history.
notes: The most relevant personal operational field after Helix. The library carries K about the operator's musical responses but is not itself a phenomenal field.
